name: Dynamic Value Codec
overview: TrustDB's Dynamic Value Codec uses format-tagged encoding to serialize values with minimal overhead. Each value carries a format tag (Bincode, JSON, CBOR, custom) and governance metadata is encoded inline rather than in separate columns. Bincode encoding adds only 3.3% overhead; JSON adds 9.2%. Governance encoding is 49x cheaper than equivalent application-layer governance.

howItWorks.title: Format Tag
howItWorks.description: Each serialized value begins with a 1-byte format tag indicating the encoding format. This enables mixed-format storage within the same tree — hot paths can use Bincode for speed while human-readable paths use JSON.

howItWorks.title: Inline Governance
howItWorks.description: Governance metadata (confidence envelope, clearance requirements, classification) is encoded as part of the value record, not in separate columns or tables. This eliminates join overhead for governance-aware reads.

howItWorks.title: Codec Selection
howItWorks.description: The codec is selected per-write based on the caller's preference or a configurable default. Reads auto-detect the format from the tag and deserialize accordingly. This enables seamless format migration.

howItWorks.title: Zero-Copy Paths
howItWorks.description: For Bincode format, the codec supports zero-copy deserialization where possible — pointers into the memory-mapped page are returned directly without copying data. This eliminates allocation overhead for read-heavy workloads.

comparisons.source: Self-describing format; slightly larger than Bincode
Extra tables, joins, validation triggers per operation

comparisons.narrative: The Dynamic Value Codec's main innovation is not the serialization format itself (Bincode is a known Rust serialization library) but the inline governance encoding. By embedding confidence, clearance, and classification directly in the value encoding, TrustDB avoids the join tax that any application-layer governance system must pay. The 49x improvement over app-layer governance is the key benchmark.

competitors.approach: Binary JSON with decomposed key storage for indexing
competitors.limitation: No inline governance; separate columns for metadata; higher encoding overhead
No governance encoding; application must manage separate metadata fields

competitors.approach: Opaque byte arrays; application handles serialization
competitors.limitation: No codec assistance; application manages all encoding including governance
Schema-dependent; no governance awareness; requires code generation
